title: Alternanthera urbani
taxonomy: Alternanthera urbani is classified within the family Amaranthaceae. This classification places it in the order Caryophyllales. The genus Alternanthera is further classified within the tribe Amarantheae. Alternanthera urbani is native to tropical regions and is often found growing in wet environments. The exact classification of Alternanthera urbani may vary depending on the source, but it is generally accepted to be a member of the Amaranthaceae family.

intro: Alternanthera urbani, also known as 'Mangrove Joyweed', is a tropical plant species native to the coastal regions of Central and South America, characterized by its small, oval-shaped leaves and vibrant red or purple stems. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and fever, and as an antiseptic to treat wounds and infections. The bioactive constituents of Alternanthera urbani include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include infusions and decoctions, often used to treat respiratory issues, skin conditions, and other health problems.
